title: Canadian Environmental Protection Act: virtual elimination list - Canada.ca
content:
## Canadian Environmental Protection Act: virtual elimination list
Section 65 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 (CEPA) mandates the Ministers of Environment and Climate Change and of Health to compile a list to be known as the virtual elimination list. The Ministers must specify the level of quantification for each substance on the list and, having done so, must prescribe the quantity or concentration of the substance that may be released into the environment either alone or in combination with any other substance from any source or type of source.
Virtual elimination list: updated February 4, 2009
- Virtual Elimination List
- Regulations Adding Perfluorooctane Sulfonate and Its Salts to the Virtual Elimination List
